Guideline: Define services with tonic and protobuf
Description: Use .proto files to define gRPC services; generate Rust code with tonic-build in build.rs
Do: Define messages and services in .proto; use tonic-build in build.rs; organize proto files by domain
Dont: Manually define message types; skip code generation

Description: Implement the generated trait for your service; compose services with tonic's Server
Do: Implement the generated trait; use tonic::Status for errors; add tower middleware layers; configure TLS
Dont: Return generic errors; skip health checking; ignore TLS for production

Description: Map domain errors to appropriate gRPC status codes with descriptive messages
Do: Use Status::not_found for 404; Status::invalid_argument for validation; Status::internal for server errors; add metadata for details
Dont: Use Status::internal for all errors; include stack traces in status messages

Description: Use tonic streaming for large result sets or real-time data feeds
Do: Return a stream via tokio_stream; use mpsc channel for producer/consumer; handle client disconnection gracefully
Dont: Load all data into memory before sending

Description: Use tonic interceptors to verify auth tokens before requests reach the service
Do: Extract metadata; verify tokens; reject unauthenticated requests with Status::unauthenticated
Dont: Skip auth on gRPC services; hardcode credentials
